description_long: This object is a vintage wooden stereo sound system cabinet, featuring a rich, polished wood veneer exterior with a rectangular form and rounded edges. The front section has two doors that open to reveal a classic analog radio dial, two control knobs, and a speaker grille below, while the top lid lifts to expose a turntable for playing vinyl records. The lower front panel is covered with a fabric speaker grille, divided vertically, and the cabinet stands on a slightly raised base. The sides are smooth and unadorned, showcasing the wood grain, while the back is made of a perforated black panel for ventilation and access to internal components. The overall design is reminiscent of mid-20th-century home audio furniture, blending functionality with decorative appeal. This stereo system would typically be found in living rooms, vintage shops, or as a decorative piece in urban environments.
description: A vintage wooden stereo sound system cabinet with a turntable on top, analog radio controls, and a fabric speaker grille, all housed in a polished wood exterior.
description_view_0: Front view showing the open top lid revealing the turntable, open front doors exposing the radio dial, control knobs, and the fabric speaker grille below.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the smooth, polished wood grain and the open top lid, with the front doors partially visible.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the black perforated panel for ventilation and access, with the top lid open.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the wood grain, open top lid, and partially open front doors.
attribute_speaker_grille_pattern: The speaker grille is covered with a vertically divided beige fabric, with no visible geometric or decorative patterns.
attribute_control_panel_layout: The control panel consists of a central analog radio dial flanked by two round control knobs, all located behind the front doors above the speaker grille.

license_info.creator_display_name: Museum of Engineering and Technology, Krakow
